titleStmt.title: Letter from J. C. Simpson to Henry Arthur Wynne, 7 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: J. C. Simpson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
46/W/1046 War Office, London S.W. 7th June 1916 Sir, With reference to War Office letter of the
20th July 1916, No. 46/690 (Accounts 3.) I am directed
to transmit the enclosed documents which have been for-
warded to this Department by the Board of Customs and
Excise in connection with a claim for a dependants allow-
ance for the mother of the soldier named in the
margin, and to request that you will be so good as to take
the necessary action with a view to the prosecution of the
soldier and the dependants in accordance with instructions
contained in the letter quoted. It has been ascertained that the soldier is
serving at Athlone Ireland. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, J. Simpson
for the Assistant Financial Secretary.
Solicitor General
Please advise as to a
prosecution!
June
10.6.16
Prosecute soldier and dependants
as the War Office think both should be prosecuted. My
own view in their cases is that the dependants also should be
prosecuted, Sir.
13.6.16 Regimental No. 119431 Name Gar C. Walsh Unit Royal Field Artillery
